name: Pork Chops Italiano
instructions:
1. Slice the mushrooms into even pieces. Heat a small amount of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until they release their moisture and become tender and golden, about 5-7 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

2. Add more olive oil to the same skillet and increase heat to medium-high. Pat the pork chops dry with paper towels for better browning. Place the chops in the hot skillet and sear without moving them for 3-5 minutes per side until a golden-brown crust forms. The chops do not need to be fully cooked at this stage. Transfer to a plate.

3. Reduce heat to medium. Chop the onion and mince the garlic. Add them to the skillet with any remaining oil, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Cook, stirring frequently, until the onion softens and becomes translucent, about 5 minutes.

4. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, dried basil, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to combine all the flavors.

5. Return the pork chops to the skillet, nestling them into the tomato sauce. Spoon some sauce over the tops of the chops. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer gently until the pork is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 63 degrees Celsius, about 45 minutes. The meat should be tender and easily pierced with a fork.

6. Slice the bell peppers into strips. Arrange the pepper strips over each pork chop, then scatter the reserved mushrooms around and on top. Cover and continue simmering for 5-10 minutes until the peppers are tender but still have a slight bite.

7. Serve the pork chops hot, spooning the rich tomato sauce and vegetables over the top.
simpleInstructions:
1. Sauté mushrooms until golden and set aside.

2. Sear pork chops until browned and set aside.

3. Cook onion and garlic, then add tomatoes and seasonings.

4. Simmer pork chops in sauce until fully cooked.

5. Add peppers and mushrooms, cook until tender.

6. Serve with sauce spooned over top.
